title: Tanarius angolensis
intro: Tanarius angolensis, also known as Tanacetum angolensis, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius angolensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tanarius angolensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Tanarius angolensis is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to Angola. The plant is commonly classified within the genus Tanarius. Further classification details are not readily available, but it is often categorized within the Angolan flora. More specific information about its classification may require further research.
